subject: Islam360 App Overview
conversation:
Subject: Islam360 App Overview

[Agent]: Islam360 (full name: Islam360: Quran, Hadith, Qibla) is the world's biggest Quran & Hadith app and the world's first Islamic search engine. It is available on Android (Google Play), iOS (App Store), Huawei (AppGallery), Windows (Microsoft Store), and macOS. It carries a 4.8/5 star rating with over 21,000 ratings. The app covers Quran reading, Hadith, Prayer Times, Qibla, Duas, Tasbeeh, 99 Names of Allah, Mosque Finder, Islamic Calendar, and Learn Quran sections.

subject: How to read Sahih Bukhari in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to read Sahih Bukhari in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I read Sahih Bukhari in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: To read Sahih Bukhari in Islam360: 1. Open the app and tap Hadith from the home screen or bottom navigation. 2. Select Sahih Al-Bukhari from the list of Hadith books. 3. Browse by chapter (Baab) or use the Search bar to find a specific Hadith. 4. You can filter by Sahih (authentic) or Zaeef (weak) classification. 5. Tap any Hadith to see the full Arabic text, Urdu translation, English translation, grade, reference number, and Takhreej. 6. Long-press or tap the bookmark icon to save a Hadith for later.

subject: All Hadith books available in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: Which Hadith books are available in Islam360?

[Customer]: What Hadith books are available in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: Islam360 contains over 78,000 Ahadith across 10+ books. The 7 primary books (Kutub al-Sittah + Musnad Ahmed) are: Sahih Al-Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Jame Tirmazi, Sunnan Abu Dawood, Sunnan Nisai, Sunnan Ibne Maja, and Musnad Ahmed. Additional books include: Al-Silsila-tus-Sahiha, Mishkat-ul-Masabeeh, Sahih Ibn Hibban, Musannif Ibn Shayba, Sahih Ibn Khuzaymah, Sunan al-Darimi, Muwatta Imam Malik, Majma Al-Zawa'id, Mustadrik Al-Hakim, and Sunnan-e-Beyhaqi. Each Hadith shows the Arabic text, Urdu and English translations, international reference number, Baab (chapter), authenticity grade, and Takhreej.

subject: How to search Hadith in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to search Hadith in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I search for a specific Hadith in Islam360?

[Agent]: To search Hadith in Islam360: Open the app and go to the Hadith section. Use the Search bar at the top — the app supports Auto Language Detection, so you can type in Arabic, Urdu, Roman Urdu, or English and it will detect the language automatically. You can also filter results by Sahih (authentic) or Zaeef (weak) to narrow down results. To find a Hadith by its number, open the specific book (e.g. Sahih Bukhari) and use the search or navigate chapter by chapter.

subject: How to read the Quran in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to read the Quran with translation in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I read the Quran with translation in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: To read the Quran with translation: 1. Open the app and tap Quran from the home screen. 2. Choose Surah listing (114 chapters by name) or Parah/Juz listing (30 sections). 3. Select your desired Surah or Parah. 4. Tap the Translation toggle to enable your preferred language. 5. To change the translator, go to Settings within the Quran view and select from 25+ translations including Muhammad Juna Garhi, Noor ul Amin, Mufti Taqi Usmani, Tahir-ul-Qadri, Abul Alaa Moududi, and others in English, Urdu, Hindi, Roman Urdu, Punjabi, Persian, Kashmiri, Seraiki, Potohari, and Gojri. 6. To enable Tafseer, tap the Tafseer option and choose a scholar such as Ibn Kathir, Maududi, or Taqi Usmani.

subject: Quran reading modes in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: What are the different Quran reading modes in Islam360?

[Customer]: What are the different ways to read Quran in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: Islam360 offers multiple Quran reading modes: 1. Standard Surah view — read verse by verse with translation. 2. Parah/Juz view — browse all 30 sections. 3. 15-Line Quran — traditional multi-verse page layout for faster reading. 4. 16-Line PDF Quran — high-quality PDF with annotation support. 5. Color-Coded Tajweed Quran — Tajweed rules highlighted visually, certified by the Research & Registration Department of Auqaf. 6. Word-by-Word translation — zoom in on each Arabic word and see its individual meaning. You can also enable Night Mode for comfortable reading in the dark.

subject: How to access Tafseer in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to access Tafseer in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I read Tafseer in Islam360?

[Agent]: To access Tafseer in Islam360: Open the Quran section, navigate to any Surah and Ayah, then tap the Tafseer option. Islam360 has 16+ Tafaseer available. Text-based Tafaseer include: Tafseer Ibn Kathir (Ibne Kaseer), Tafseer Maududi (Abul Ala Moudodi), Tafseer Taqi Usmani, and Ahsan-ul-Biyan. Audio Tafaseer include Dr. Israr Ahmed and Mufti M. Sayeed. Fahm-ul-Quran (understanding Quran through grammar) is also available as a course format.

subject: How to search the Quran in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to search in the Quran in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I search for a specific word or topic in the Quran on Islam360?

[Agent]: To search the Quran in Islam360: Tap Quran from the home screen, then tap the Search (magnifying glass) icon. You have four search options: 1. Keyword search — type in English, Urdu, Hindi, or Roman Urdu to search translations. 2. Arabic search — type Arabic text to search the original Quran. 3. Root Word — browse alphabetically ordered Arabic root words. 4. Subject/Topic — browse all Quranic topics alphabetically in English and Urdu, like a subject index. Voice search is also supported across Quran content.

subject: Quran reciters available in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: Which Qaris and reciters are available in Islam360?

[Customer]: What reciters are available for Quran audio in Islam360?

[Agent]: Islam360 offers 12+ Qaris for audio recitation including: Sheikh As-Sudais, Sheikh Al-Shuraim, Qari Abdul Basit, Saad El Ghamidi, Sheikh Hani Ar-Rifai, Sheikh Ahmed Al Ajmi, and Qari Ubaid-ur-Rehman (recently added). To switch reciters, go to the Quran section, start audio playback, and select your preferred Qari from the reciter options. Audio translations are also available in Kashmiri, Seraiki, Potohari, Gojri, and Persian.

subject: How to use Prayer Times in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to find prayer times in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I check prayer times in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: To check prayer times: Open the app and tap Prayer Times or Namaz Timings from the home screen. Allow location permissions on first use so the app can calculate accurate times for your area. You will see all 5 daily prayer times (Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, Isha) for your current location. To enable Azan notifications, tap the bell/notification icon next to each prayer. During Ramadan, Sehar and Iftar times are also displayed. You can also add a Prayer Time Widget to your phone's home screen for quick access without opening the app.

subject: How to use the Qibla finder in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to find Qibla direction in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I find the Qibla direction using Islam360?

[Agent]: To find the Qibla direction: Open the app and tap Qibla or Compass from the main menu. Hold your device flat and rotate it slowly until the compass points to the Qibla direction toward the Kaaba in Mecca. Allow location and compass permissions if prompted. This feature works globally and is especially useful when travelling.

subject: How to access Duas and Azkar in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to find Duas and Azkar in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I find Duas and supplications in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: To access Duas and Azkar: Open the app and navigate to the Ibaadaat or Azkar section from the main menu. Available categories include: Namaz Azkar (for all prayer positions — post-Takbir-Tahreema, Rukooh, Sujdah, Tashahud), Roza/Fasting duas (Sehri and Iftar), Hajj duas and guide, Umrah duas and guide, Namaze Janaza (funeral prayer), Morning and Evening Azkar, and Daily life duas (waking up, eating, travelling, illness). Each dua is shown in Arabic with Urdu and English translation. Note: some Ibadat content is currently primarily in Urdu.

subject: How to use the Tasbeeh counter in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to use the Tasbeeh counter in Islam360

[Customer]: How do I use the Tasbeeh counter in Islam360?

[Agent]: To use the Tasbeeh counter: Open the app and tap Tasbeeh from the main menu or tools section. Tap the counter button once for each dhikr repetition. Tap the reset button to start a new count. You can also add the Tasbeeh Widget to your phone's home screen by long-pressing the home screen, selecting Widgets, searching for Islam360, and adding the Tasbeeh widget for quick access.

subject: 99 Names of Allah in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: Where can I find 99 Names of Allah in Islam360?

[Customer]: How do I access the 99 names of Allah in Islam360?

[Agent]: To access the 99 Names of Allah (Asma ul Husna): Open the app and navigate to the 99 Names section from the main menu. You will find all 99 names of Allah with their meanings and attribute explanations. The app also includes the 99 names of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.). Note: one name that appears is Zul-Jalal (or Dhul-Jalal wal-Ikram) — both Zul-Jalal and Zul-Jalal wal-Ikram are used in different scholarly references, which is why you may see slight variation.

subject: How to learn Quran reading in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to learn to read Quran in Islam360

[Customer]: How can I learn to read Quran using Islam360?

[Agent]: Islam360 has a dedicated Learn Quran section. Open the app and tap Learn Quran from the main menu. Available resources: 1. Qaida — 27 structured lessons for learning Quranic basics with audio guidance. Navigate lesson by lesson. 2. Tajweed Rules — the Color-Coded Quran visually displays Tajweed rules. 3. Fahm-ul-Quran — Quranic comprehension courses understanding meaning through Arabic grammar. 4. Understand Quran Courses — detailed courses for studying Quranic meaning in context. 5. Word-by-Word Translation — see the exact meaning of each Arabic word. 6. Seerat un Nabi — video biography of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.), Season 1 and Season 2 available.

subject: How to use bookmarks in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How do I bookmark in Islam360?

[Customer]: How do I save my reading position or bookmark in Islam360?

[Agent]: To bookmark content in Islam360: For Quran — while reading, tap on any Ayah and select Bookmark to save your position or mark important verses. For Hadith — tap the bookmark icon on any Hadith card while reading. To access all your saved bookmarks, go to your profile or bookmarks section from the main menu. You can also add Notes alongside any Ayah by tapping the note option while reading.

subject: Islam360 subscription plans and pricing
conversation:
Subject: Islam360 subscription plans and pricing

[Customer]: What are the subscription options for Islam360?

[Agent]: Islam360 is free to download on all platforms. The free version includes full access to Quran, Hadith, Prayer Times, Qibla, Duas, and Tasbeeh but displays advertisements. A premium subscription removes all ads and unlocks offline reading. Subscription options (USD): Monthly at $9.99/month, Yearly at $49.99/year, Lifetime at $74.99 (one-time), Three-Year Plan at $79.99, and a Ramadan Special at $8.99. A 7-day free trial is available for new users. Subscriptions auto-renew unless cancelled at least 24 hours before the renewal date through your App Store or Google Play account settings.

subject: How to cancel Islam360 subscription
conversation:
Subject: How to cancel Islam360 subscription

[Customer]: How do I cancel my Islam360 subscription?

[Agent]: To cancel your Islam360 subscription, you need to do it through the platform you subscribed on — not through the app itself. For iOS: Go to your iPhone Settings → tap your Apple ID at the top → Subscriptions → find Islam360 → tap Cancel Subscription. For Android/Google Play: Open Google Play Store → tap your profile icon → Payments & subscriptions → Subscriptions → find Islam360 → Cancel subscription. Cancel at least 24 hours before your next billing date to avoid being charged for the next period. Your premium access continues until the end of the paid period.

subject: How to remove ads from Islam360
conversation:
Subject: How to remove ads from Islam360

[Customer]: How do I get rid of ads in the Islam360 app?

[Agent]: To remove ads from Islam360, you need to purchase a premium subscription. Options include Monthly ($9.99), Yearly ($49.99), Lifetime ($74.99), or a Three-Year Plan ($79.99). A 7-day free trial is available for new users. Once subscribed, ads are removed throughout the entire app. If you have already subscribed but are still seeing ads, please make sure you are logged into the same account used for the purchase, and try restarting the app. If the issue persists, please contact our support team.

subject: How to sync Islam360 subscription across devices
conversation:
Subject: How to sync Islam360 subscription across multiple devices

[Customer]: I paid on my iPad but I still see ads on my iPhone. How do I sync my subscription?

[Agent]: To sync your Islam360 subscription across devices, make sure you are signed in with the same Apple ID or Google account on all your devices. For iOS: go to Settings in the app and tap Restore Purchases. This will apply your existing subscription to the current device. If the issue persists after restoring, please contact our support team with your purchase receipt and we will assist you.

subject: Offline mode in Islam360
conversation:
Subject: Can I use Islam360 offline?

[Customer]: Can I use Islam360 without internet? Does it work offline?

[Agent]: Offline reading is available with the premium subscription. The free version generally requires an internet connection to load content. With a premium subscription, you can download content for offline access. If you have subscribed and are having trouble accessing content offline, please ensure the content has been downloaded while connected to the internet, then try accessing it offline.

subject: Which platforms is Islam360 available on
conversation:
Subject: Which platforms and devices support Islam360?

[Customer]: Which devices and platforms can I use Islam360 on?

[Agent]: Islam360 is available on: Android (download from Google Play Store, search Islam360 or use package ID com.islam360), iOS (download from Apple App Store, works on iPhone, iPad, iPod touch running iOS 12.0 or later), Huawei devices (download from Huawei AppGallery), Windows (available on Microsoft Store as Islam360 Universal, requires Windows 8.1 or later), and macOS (available via Apple App Store). There is currently no dedicated Linux or web browser version.

subject: How to set up Islam360 widgets
conversation:
Subject: How to add Islam360 widgets to home screen

[Customer]: How do I add Islam360 widgets to my phone's home screen?

[Agent]: To add Islam360 widgets: Long-press on an empty area of your phone's home screen. Select Widgets from the menu that appears. Search for Islam360 in the widget list. Available widgets include: Verse of the Day, Hadith of the Day, Supplication of the Day, Daily Inspiration, Tasbeeh Counter, Prayer Time, and Sehar & Iftar Time. Tap and hold the widget you want, then drag it to your desired position on the home screen.

subject: Common Islam360 app issues and fixes
conversation:
Subject: Common Islam360 troubleshooting

[Customer]: The Islam360 app is not working properly.

[Agent]: Here are solutions to common Islam360 issues: 1. Audio not playing — check your device volume and audio settings, then close and restart the app. 2. App lagging or jumping pages in 15-Line Quran — close other apps running in the background and restart Islam360. 3. Subscription not showing or ads still appearing after purchase — go to Settings in the app and tap Restore Purchases, ensure you are on the same account used for purchase. 4. Tafseer switching glitch on iPad — close and reopen the app. 5. App crashing — ensure you have the latest version installed from your app store. If the issue continues, please contact our support team at islam360helpcenter@gmail.com.

subject: AI360 and WhatsApp AI feature in Islam360

[Agent]: AI360 is an AI-powered feature introduced in Islam360 version 4.7.4 that enhances the app's Islamic search and content discovery capabilities. It is also available as a widget for Daily Inspiration and Supplication. Additionally, Islam360 has recently added a WhatsApp AI feature (version 21.1.2) which allows you to get AI-assisted Islamic query responses through WhatsApp integration. These features position Islam360 as an AI-enhanced Islamic search engine.

The app will display nearby mosques and Islamic centers. This feature works globally and is especially useful when you are travelling or in an unfamiliar area.

subject: Islamic Hijri calendar in Islam360

[Agent]: Yes, Islam360 includes an Islamic Calendar feature that displays the current Hijri (Islamic lunar) date alongside the Gregorian date. You can access it from the main menu. The current Islamic date is also shown on the home screen of the app at all times.
